channel.title: Opensuse on ilManzo's blog
channel.description: Recent content in Opensuse on ilManzo's blog

item.title: A Friendly Guide to Podman Quadlets
item.description:
🦎 Hi geekos!

If you’ve been running containers on your Leap or Tumbleweed machine, you probably started with podman run commands. Maybe you moved to Docker Compose files to manage stacks. Those are great tools, but they have a limitation, as they don’t integrate natively with systemd, your operating system’s init system.

When your server reboots, do your containers start back up automatically? If a container crashes, does it restart? How do you view its logs alongside your system journal?
